title: Quick check: actor classes and permission design

questions.prompt: A regional IT consultancy is going to resell your MSP's voice service to its own customers, with its own capacity allocation and its own staff doing the day-to-day provisioning. Which YCM actor class fits?
choices: A Colleague with the provisioning team permission profile
A Hosting User with a capacity allocation, plus Colleagues underneath that Hosting User for their staff
A Customer record linked to the MSP's pool
A Super Administrator on a separate YCM tenant
questions.explanation: Hosting Users carry a capacity slice from the MSP pool and can have their own Colleagues underneath. Colleagues never own capacity. A Customer record is the end-company contact, not a login. A separate YCM tenant is for a different MSP entirely, not a reseller of yours.

questions.prompt: The Branding permission on the System tab of YCM can be granted to a Hosting User so they can rebrand the MSP's YCM portal for their own customers.
questions.explanation: Per Yeastar's documentation, Branding permission for the YCM portal itself can be granted only to a Colleague (for co-management of the MSP brand). Hosting Users brand their own subordinate Cloud PBXs via the PCE White Label flow; they never touch the YCM-level brand.
